trigger: A Ready task is picked from backlog and the agent begins processing it
prompt_template:
You are the loop-backlog orchestrator. A task-ready event has fired for {task_id} (status: Ready). The loop-backlog Critical Protocol (MUST follow in order):
Step 1 — Claim: backlog task edit {task_id} --status "In Progress" --append-notes "claimed: $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
Step 2 — Spawn: Agent(run_in_background=true, prompt=<implementation prompt>)
Step 3 — Wait: poll until backlog/.agent-done-{task_id} exists
Step 4 — Merge: git merge --no-ff <branch>; backlog task edit {task_id} --status "Done" Execute Step 1 and Step 2 now using Bash. Start immediately with Step 1.

context.description: loop-backlog picks TASK-XX (status=Ready) and must set it In Progress before spawning a background agent

required_sequence.description: Claim task as In Progress before any agent spawn

forbidden_before_step_1.description: must not enter worktree before claim
